novel_features.name: Stale leads
novel_features.description: Find leads not touched in N days across every campaign at once.
novel_features.rationale: Smartlead's API has no global lead view; cross-campaign staleness requires a local join across leads, messages, and last-event-at.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to sweep neglected leads across an entire account without paginating every campaign individually.

novel_features.name: Overlap leads
novel_features.description: Surface leads that exist in multiple campaigns (duplicate outreach risk).
novel_features.rationale: Smartlead's API only resolves leads one email at a time; cross-campaign dedup needs a local index of every lead.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before adding a new lead list to confirm no contact is already mid-sequence somewhere else.

novel_features.name: Mailbox burnout
novel_features.description: Rank email accounts approaching warmup decline or sending-limit ceiling.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a snapshot history of warmup-stats and sending-limits; the API only returns point-in-time values.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to pause at-risk senders before deliverability craters or 60/min rate-limits trigger 429s.

novel_features.name: Reply velocity
novel_features.description: Time-to-first-reply per campaign with historical baseline.
novel_features.rationale: Requires snapshotted historical analytics rolled up across campaigns; API gives current snapshot only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to spot which campaigns are speeding up or slowing down vs their own baseline.

novel_features.name: Deliverability drift
novel_features.description: Compare today vs 7d vs 30d spam-test placement per sender domain.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local snapshot history of spam-test results; API returns single-test snapshots only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to catch deliverability decline before it becomes a crisis.

novel_features.name: Reach budget
novel_features.description: Days-to-completion per campaign from current sending-limits and pending leads.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining email-account daily limits with campaign rotation pool and pending lead count.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to set realistic delivery expectations for a sales team or to size how many senders a campaign needs.

novel_features.name: Agency roll-up
novel_features.description: Aggregate stats across all client API keys, per-client and portfolio-wide.
novel_features.rationale: Multi-key fan-out + local aggregation; the API forces agencies to run per-client manually.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when a portfolio owner wants a single view across every client account.

novel_features.name: Reply classify
novel_features.description: Classify master-inbox replies (positive / objection / OOO / unsub) using local FTS + keywords.
novel_features.rationale: Smartlead's built-in categorization is coarse; a local classifier with FTS over reply bodies is much faster and scriptable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to triage hundreds of replies into action buckets without reading each one.

narrative.headline: Every Smartlead feature, plus offline sync, FTS5 search, auto-chunking, and cross-campaign aggregations no other Smartlead tool has.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI mirrors all 155 Smartlead endpoints as agent-native commands and adds a local SQLite store so cross-campaign queries (stale, overlap, mailbox-burnout, deliv-drift, agency roll-up) run in milliseconds without re-paginating. Every command supports --json, --select, --csv, and a typed exit-code palette so it composes with jq and shell pipelines.
narrative.auth_narrative: Smartlead authenticates by query string. Set `SMARTLEAD_API_KEY=<your-admin-or-client-key>` in your environment, or run `smartlead-pp-cli auth set-token` to store it. The CLI honors Smartlead's 60 requests/minute budget per key with an adaptive limiter that surfaces a typed RateLimitError when exhausted instead of returning empty results.

quickstart.comment: Confirm auth + reachability before anything else.
List every campaign on the account.
Hydrate the local SQLite store so transcendence commands can run offline.
Find leads neglected for two weeks across every campaign at once.
Rank senders by burnout risk so you can pause the at-risk ones.

troubleshoots.fix: The CLI already throttles to 60/min; if you're running multiple shells, serialize them or split workload across client API keys.
The CLI auto-chunks leads in batches of 400; if you see this, you're on an older build — rerun `go install`.
Run `export SMARTLEAD_API_KEY=<key>` or `smartlead-pp-cli auth set-token`. Check `smartlead-pp-cli doctor` for resolution.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to operate across many Smartlead campaigns or clients at once, when the workflow demands cross-campaign aggregation that the Smartlead UI and API don't expose, or when you need fast offline search over leads and replies. It also fits any Smartlead automation pipeline that benefits from typed exit codes, structured JSON, and adaptive rate-limit handling.

recipes.title: Find stale leads with selected fields
recipes.explanation: Project only the columns the agent needs; --agent emits compact JSON without ANSI.

recipes.title: Trace duplicate outreach
recipes.explanation: Surface every lead present in multiple campaigns so you can dedupe before importing more.

recipes.title: Triage replies into action buckets
recipes.explanation: Run a local FTS+keyword pass over the master-inbox to cluster replies.

recipes.title: Bulk lead import without 400-cap pain
recipes.explanation: The CLI auto-chunks anything over 400 transparently.
